mission.title: Install and Configure Flux on Kubernetes
mission.description: Flux is an open and extensible continuous delivery solution for Kubernetes, designed to keep your clusters in sync with configuration sources like Git repositories. Installing Flux allows you to automate updates to your Kubernetes configurations in a GitOps manner.

steps.title: Add the Flux Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Flux Helm repository to your local Helm client to access the Flux charts.
```bash
helm repo add fluxcd https://charts.fluxcd.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Flux using Helm
steps.description:
Install Flux in the 'flux-system' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist. This command installs Flux version 2.8.1.
```bash
helm install flux fluxcd/flux --version v2.8.1 --namespace flux-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Flux components are running correctly by listing the pods in the 'flux-system' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

steps.title: Configure Flux for GitOps
steps.description:
Set up Flux to sync with your Git repository. Replace <GIT_REPO_URL> and <GIT_BRANCH> with your repository details.
```bash
flux bootstrap github --owner=<GITHUB_OWNER> --repository=<GIT_REPO_URL> --branch=<GIT_BRANCH> --path=clusters/my-cluster
```

steps.title: Set resource limits for Flux components
steps.description:
Apply resource limits to the Flux components to ensure they run efficiently. Create a YAML file named `flux-resources.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flux
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: flux
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "512Mi"
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "256Mi"
```
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f flux-resources.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Flux Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Flux release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall flux --namespace flux-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Flux Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove the Flux CRDs that were created during installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep fluxcd.io | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Flux namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the 'flux-system' namespace to clean up all resources associated with Flux.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace flux-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current Flux configuration
upgrade.description:
Export the current Flux configuration to a YAML file for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace flux-system -o yaml > flux-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Flux Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Flux Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flux to the latest version
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Flux to the latest version available in the repository.
```bash
helm upgrade flux fluxcd/flux --namespace flux-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Flux pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see Flux pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace flux-system
```
Common issues include misconfigured Git repository access or resource limits being too low. Adjust configurations as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Flux not syncing with Git repository
troubleshooting.description:
If Flux is not syncing, check the status of the Git repository:
```bash
flux get gitrepositories --namespace flux-system
```
Ensure that the repository URL and credentials are correct. Update the configuration if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release failed to install
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, check for existing resources that may conflict:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace flux-system
```
Remove any conflicting resources or try a different release name.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Flux will have all components running in the 'flux-system' namespace, with the Flux pods in a 'Running' state. You should also be able to see the Flux configuration syncing with your specified Git repository.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add fluxcd https://charts.fluxcd.io
helm repo update
helm install flux fluxcd/flux --version v2.8.1 --namespace flux-system --create-namespace

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flux
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: flux
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "512Mi"
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
